data.narrative:
Mistral AI is a private-stage AI & Software company seeking valuation assessment against a universe of 9 comparable companies: 8 private peers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Anysphere, Neuralink, Groq, and others) and 1 public comparable (PLTR).

The peer-median method is applicable and forms the primary triangulation approach. Across the 5-company same-sector, same-stage peer set, the median valuation stands at $29.3B, with a range spanning $7.0B (Groq) to $300.0B (OpenAI). This dispersion reflects heterogeneity in model capability, deployment scale, and funding trajectory within frontier AI. Mistral's current €11.7B valuation (~$12.7B USD equivalent) sits materially below the peer median, despite $14B in total capital raised.

The triangulated valuation range is $24.9B to $33.7B, centered on a $29.3B median. Mistral's present valuation implies a discount of approximately 57% to the peer median. Key uncertainties include (1) the appropriateness of the OpenAI and Anthropic comparables, given their substantially higher valuations and strategic positioning; (2) revenue traction and unit economics relative to the peer set, which the data does not capture; and (3) the wide dispersion in the peer universe itself, which limits precision of any point estimate. A single-method triangulation warrants caution; additional revenue multiple or DCF analysis would strengthen the assessment.
